title: Auto Air Conditioner Symptom Diagnostic Table

crossRefs.label: Inside/outside air switching motor does not operate
Auto A/C control unit does not work at all
A/C system does not work (radiator fan and compressor do not work.)

blocks.text: Auto Air Conditioner Symptom Diagnostic Table

rows.text: Inside/outside air switching motor does not operate

refs.label: Inside/outside air switching motor does not operate

rows.text: Inspection of inside/outside air switching motor operating circuit
Poor wiring, coupler, or terminal connections Defective inside/outside air switching motor or auto A/C control unit
Auto A/C control unit does not work at all

refs.label: Auto A/C control unit does not work at all

rows.text: Inspect the auto A/C control unit operating circuit.
Defective No. C26 (7.5A) fuse in fuse box Defective body grounding (G403) Poor wiring, coupler, or terminal connections Defective auto A/C control unit

Defective No. A19 (7.5A) fuse in relay box Poor wiring, coupler, or terminal connections Compressor, compressor clutch, compressor clutch thermal protector, compressor clutch relay, or PGM-FI control system failure
A/C system does not work (radiator fan and compressor do not work.)

refs.label: A/C system does not work (radiator fan and compressor do not work.)

rows.text: PGM-FI control system and A/C system inspection
Poor wiring, coupler, or terminal connections Defective A/C pressure switch, evaporator sensor, auto A/C control unit, or PGM-FI control system
HDS does not communicate with auto A/C control unit or vehicle
